title: Indicator catalogue (canonical)
description: Hand-authored canonical indicator catalogue (taxonomy/indicators.json). One row per indicator_id. Carries the full Hans honesty surface (D15) plus D29 catalogue additions (parent_indicator_id self-FK, dimension_values:STRUCT for facet-explode children, per-child source_id, methodology_version FK). id field enforces the D30 naming convention. Pipeline compiles to taxonomy/indicators.parquet. NOTE: this is the canonical replacement for the pre-pivot datasets/schemas/indicator.schema.json (which validates per-shard JSON under datasets/indicators/in/). The legacy file stays in place through Phase 0.13; rename of this file to indicator.schema.json happens when the pre-pivot artifacts move to _old/.

x-changelog.description: Initial canonical indicator catalogue schema (Phase 0.3 of TODO/20260517-canonical-long-format-pivot.md). Carries D15 honesty surface + D29 additions: parent_indicator_id self-FK for facet-explode parents/children (D26), dimension_values:STRUCT populated iff parent_indicator_id IS NOT NULL, per-child source_id (D29 — siblings can have different upstreams), methodology_version FK to methodology-breaks.json (D28). id pattern enforces D30 kebab-case, single-segment, <=60 chars. dimension_values keys MUST appear in taxonomy/facet-axes.json registry (D31 — enforced by validator, not by JSON Schema). coverage_* columns are denormalised at every emit so the catalogue stays browseable from DuckDB-WASM without scanning observation Parquet.

indicator_id.description: PK. D30 naming convention: <entity>-<measure>-<unit>-<facet>, kebab-case, single segment, max 60 chars. Approved abbreviations: nsdp, gsdp, cpi, imr, mmr, tfr, mw, gwh, mu, inr, pct. New abbreviations require Max sign-off. Methodology-version children encode the version (e.g. 'state-gsdp-base-2011-12-inr-crore'). Greppable; deterministic; no hash.

description_short.description: One-line summary of what the indicator IS. OWID separates label from description.

description_long.description: Paragraph-form definition + methodology pointer. Surfaced on the indicator page.

cadence.description: Per ADR-0027 + D15. Publisher's release cadence. decennial / ad_hoc series have no defined inter-observation interval — derive_temporal_range() omits gap_count for these.

default_period_seq_for_cadence.description: When multiple cadences coexist for this indicator (e.g. monthly + annual), the period_seq the chart picks by default. Null when not applicable.

family.description: Storage axis (e.g. 'elections', 'energy', 'fiscal'). Partition column when family Parquet > 15 MB (D8).

pillar.description: Curation axis for the citizen-facing browse. Distinct from family (storage axis).

denominator.description: For rates/ratios: what they are per (e.g. 'population_total', 'gsdp_current_inr_crore'). Free-text; may be an indicator_id or a publisher-defined denominator.

attribution_geography.description: What the entity_id geographically represents per Hans review. Renderer surfaces banner for where_produced / where_allocated / where_billed to prevent siting-as-service misreading.

comparability.description: Hans 4-level honesty ladder. directional_only suppresses rank-table views and labels the choropleth 'illustrative, not a ranking'.

implementing_authority.description: Who actually executes vs who funds (see funding_split). Many CSS schemes are centre-funded but state-implemented = joint.

funding_split.description: Centre-vs-state attribution. Critical so a citizen reading 'state X is doing well' knows whether the state government, the Centre, or a CSS drove it.

methodology_vintage.description: Short string naming the methodology revision (e.g. 'GSDP base 2011-12'). Distinct from observation year — a 2024 NFHS-6 figure has the same year as a 2024 NFHS-5 backcast but different vintage.

revision_tier.description: Publisher's revision-tier discipline for this indicator. 'mixed' when rows span tiers (each row carries its own vintage on the observation).

excluded_notes.description: Citizen-facing what's-NOT-counted bullets (Hans framing). e.g. 'IGNOAPS / IGNWPS social pensions excluded'.

parent_indicator_id.description: D26 facet-explode self-FK. null for parent indicators; populated for facet-explode children. When non-null, dimension_values MUST be non-null (validator-enforced).

dimension_values.description: D29. Populated iff parent_indicator_id IS NOT NULL. Keys MUST appear in taxonomy/facet-axes.json registry (D31 — validator-enforced, not JSON Schema). e.g. {fuel_type: 'coal'} for a 'state-installed-capacity-mw-coal' child of 'state-installed-capacity-mw'.

methodology_version.description: D28. FK to methodology-breaks.json. Together with id-encoded break (D30 puts the version in the id) gives the chart shell both the splice-marker (visible vertical rule via D32) and the prose narrative.

methodology_break_ids.description: FK[] -> methodology-breaks.json. All breaks active across this series (a long series can cross multiple).

source_id.description: D29 — per-child source_id (NOT shared across siblings). coal-capacity from CEA and solar-capacity from MNRE are siblings of one parent with different sources. null on the parent row; required on each child.

coverage_states_count.description: Denormalised at every emit. Supports the catalogue's 'has data for >=80% states' filter without scanning observation Parquet from the browser.

coverage_year_min.description: Denormalised. Earliest observation year for this indicator.

coverage_density.description: Denormalised. Fraction of (eligible_entity x year) cells observed. 1.0 = complete; 0.0 = empty.

renderer_rules.description: Controlled-vocab slugs binding renderer behaviour (e.g. 'no_rank_table', 'no_growth_across_break', 'mask_be_in_long_view'). Vocabulary lives in docs/concepts/indicator-naming.md.
